sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal.-

(1) to bind, comp. Piel and אֲלֻמָּה.

(2) passive, to be bound, sc. the tongue, i.e. to be dumb, to be silent; see Niph. and the nouns אֵלֶם, אִלֵּם, אַלְמֹנִי. To be silent, dumb, as if tongue-tied (δεσμὸς τῆς γλώσσης, Mar 7:35), comp. Pers. زبن بستن to bind the tongue, for to be silent, and Gr. φιμοῦσθαι.

(3) to be solitary, forsaken, widowed, for a solitary person is silent as he has no companion with whom to talk; comp. بكم to be mute, to be unmarried. Hence are derived אַלְמָן, אַלְמָנָה, אַלְמֹן, אַלְמָנוּה.

Niphal.-

(1) to be dumb, mute, Psa 31:19 39:3 , 10 Psa 39:10 Isa 53:7.

(2) to be silent, Eze 33:22.

Piel, to bind together, Gen 37:7.
